children.heading: CITES
children.label: 1 CITES
children.text: The term “CITES” means the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora, done at Washington (27 UST 1087; TIAS 8249), including its appendices.March 3, 1973

children.label: A
children.text: means the use of methods and procedures necessary to prevent the diminution of, and to sustain viable populations of, a species; and

children.label: i
children.text: conservation, protection, restoration, acquisition, and management of habitat;

children.label: ii
children.text: in-situ research and monitoring of populations and habitats;

children.label: iii
children.text: assistance in the development, implementation, and improvement of management plans for managed habitat ranges;

children.label: v
children.text: enforcement and implementation of domestic laws relating to resource management;

children.label: vi
children.text: development and operation of sanctuaries for members of a species rescued from the illegal trade in live animals;

children.label: vii
children.text: training of local law enforcement officials in the interdiction and prevention of the illegal killing of great apes;

children.label: viii
children.text: programs for the rehabilitation of members of a species in the wild and release of the members into the wild in ways which do not threaten existing wildlife populations by causing displacement or the introduction of disease;

children.label: xi
children.text: strengthening the capacity of local communities to implement conservation programs.

children.heading: Fund
children.label: 3 Fund
children.text: The term “Fund” means the Great Ape Conservation Fund established by .section 6304 of this title

children.heading: Great ape
children.label: 4 Great ape
children.text: The term “great ape” means a chimpanzee, gorilla, bonobo, orangutan, or gibbon.

children.heading: Multinational Species Conservation Fund
children.label: 5 Multinational Species Conservation Fund
children.text: The term “Multinational Species Conservation Fund” means such fund as established in title I of the Department of the Interior and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 1999, under the heading “” [].multinational species conservation fund16 U.S.C. 4246

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 6 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.
